raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general request to look at the paragraph centrally includes checking whether the argumentative progression from model features to benchmark results to adoption claims is coherent.","R1.3":"Because the paragraph reports specific benchmark outcomes and draws performance claims from them, claim-evidence alignment is a central responsibility.","R1.6":"The delegation targets a paragraph, so sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, concision, and flow are central expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"Any projection of a vague review request should preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and type of review.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong claims such as state-of-the-art advancement and wide-margin dominance, making overclaim avoidance active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually review, revise, or evaluate the paragraph.","RX.4":"The clinical-laboratory adoption context makes avoidance of downstream harm from unsupported or overstated implications active.","RX.5":"Responsibility assignments should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general request to look at the paragraph centrally includes checking whether the argumentative progression from model features to benchmark results to adoption claims is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Because the paragraph reports specific benchmark outcomes and draws performance claims from them, claim-evidence alignment is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation targets a paragraph, so sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, concision, and flow are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague review request should preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and type of review.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims such as state-of-the-art advancement and wide-margin dominance, making overclaim avoidance active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually review, revise, or evaluate the paragraph.
rationale.RX.4: The clinical-laboratory adoption context makes avoidance of downstream harm from unsupported or overstated implications active.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility assignments should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
